intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: Registry vs. Reality: P1 (Yellow Pot) is present but appears devoid of visible foliage. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single, struggling seedling/stem. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor and a single leaf. Registry is largely non-compliant with current visual evidence.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil, no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil, no signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf remaining, appears stable but isolated.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil, no signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3/P4 area; electronic component (pin header) present on desk surface.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, the primary specimen in the black pot (P3/P4) has undergone significant postural collapse. The leaf has transitioned from a horizontal, turgid state to a downward-curving, wilting state. P1 remains consistently empty.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical decline. The lack of foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests a failure of the initial plantings. The remaining leaf in P3 is showing signs of senescence or severe stress.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of an electronic component (pin header) on the desk; presence of eggshell fragments in the soil; significant discrepancy between registry and physical reality.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image stack. My first action was to map the physical pots to the registry, followed by a pixel-level inspection of the soil and foliage. I validated these findings by comparing the earliest image to the current state. The biome is failing; the registry is largely aspirational, as most specimens have suffered systemic loss. The remaining plant matter is in poor condition, likely due to environmental stressors or improper substrate management.
